name: Lenny Rachitsky
title: Founder, Lenny's Newsletter & Podcast
bio_short: Former Airbnb PM. Runs the most-read product management newsletter and podcast. Known for rigorous, practitioner-grounded takes on what actually works in product.

name: Shreyas Doshi
title: Former PM Leader, Stripe · Twitter · Google
bio_short: One of the most precise thinkers on product craft. Known for frameworks that hold under pressure — especially on how AI reshapes what PM skills actually matter long-term.

name: Gibson Biddle
title: Former VP Product, Netflix · CPO, Chegg
bio_short: Built product at Netflix and Chegg. Now teaches, writes, and runs workshops on product strategy — best known for his DHM framework and how consumer product strategy actually works.

name: Marty Cagan
title: Founder, Silicon Valley Product Group
bio_short: Author of Inspired and Empowered. The canonical voice on what separates strong product organizations from feature factories — increasingly relevant as AI reshapes team structure.

name: Julie Zhuo
title: Co-founder, Sundial · Former VP Design, Meta
bio_short: Spent 14 years at Facebook rising to VP of Product Design. Now co-founding Sundial, an AI startup. Author of The Making of a Manager — increasingly focused on how AI changes design leadership.

name: Andrej Karpathy
title: Founder, Eureka Labs · Ex-OpenAI · Ex-Tesla AI
bio_short: OpenAI co-founder, former Tesla AI Director. Now running Eureka Labs, an AI education company. Coined 'vibe coding.' The clearest explainer of where AI is actually heading, technically.

name: Sam Altman
title: CEO, OpenAI
bio_short: CEO of OpenAI. Unavoidable for understanding the macro AI trajectory. Reads best on long time horizons — his writing on intelligence, abundance, and the future of software is the strategic backdrop for everything else.

name: Ethan Mollick
title: Professor, Wharton · Author, Co-Intelligence
bio_short: The most rigorous academic voice on how AI changes knowledge work and organizations. Empirical, measured, and consistently ahead of the hype — essential reading for anyone leading in the AI era.

name: Simon Willison
title: Creator, Datasette · Independent AI Practitioner
bio_short: Prolific open-source developer and one of the sharpest, most honest practitioners writing about AI tools. Cuts through hype with working code and real-world tests — valuable for technical grounding.

name: Fabricio Teixeira
title: Founder, UX Collective
bio_short: Runs UX Collective, one of the most-read design publications. Weekly synthesis of what's shifting in design practice — currently focused on how AI and vibe coding are eroding (and reshaping) design authority.

name: Jesse James Garrett
title: AI Transformation Strategist · Co-founder, Adaptive Path
bio_short: Author of The Elements of User Experience and co-founder of Adaptive Path. Now working as an AI transformation strategist and private leadership advisor — a UX canon voice navigating the AI inflection.

name: Adam Wathan
title: Creator, Tailwind CSS
bio_short: Creator of Tailwind CSS — the design system toolkit that became ubiquitous. Influential on how design and engineering converge, and how UI decisions scale across teams.

name: Nneka Titilayo
title: UX Designer · AI × Inclusive Design Voice
bio_short: An emerging voice at the intersection of AI, UX, and inclusive design — tracking how AI-native interfaces risk encoding old biases and what it takes to design for everyone.

name: Patrick McKenzie (patio11)
title: Advisor, Stripe · Author, Bits About Money
bio_short: Advisor at Stripe. Writes Bits About Money — the sharpest newsletter on the technical and regulatory plumbing underneath payments and financial infrastructure. Required reading if you work in this space.

name: Nik Milanović
title: Founder, This Week in Fintech · GP, The Fintech Fund
bio_short: Founded This Week in Fintech, the most widely read fintech newsletter. Former Google Pay and Petal. Now a GP at The Fintech Fund — practitioner-level signal on where the industry is actually moving.

name: Simon Taylor
title: Founder, Fintech Brainfood · Advisor, Sardine
bio_short: Creator of Fintech Brainfood — consistently voted one of the most influential voices in banking and fintech. Writes weekly on payments, banking infrastructure, and the AI layer being built on top of it.

name: Angela Strange
title: General Partner, a16z · AI × Fintech
bio_short: General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz, focused on AI Applications and Fintech/Insurance. Board-level view on where capital is flowing in financial services — useful for understanding where the industry is heading, not just where it is.

name: Melissa Perri
title: Author, Escaping the Build Trap · CPO Advisor
bio_short: Author of Escaping the Build Trap. Advises CPOs and product organizations on how to build for outcomes, not output. Increasingly focused on how AI changes product operating models at scale.

name: Teresa Torres
title: Author, Continuous Discovery Habits
bio_short: Defined continuous discovery as a discipline. Most practically useful voice on how to build fast, lightweight feedback loops — especially relevant as AI makes it cheaper to build and easier to validate.

name: April Dunford
title: Author, Obviously Awesome · Positioning Expert
bio_short: The definitive voice on product positioning. Author of Obviously Awesome and Sales Pitch. Particularly relevant for anyone repositioning themselves or a product — cuts through vague strategy to executable narrative.

name: Josh Woodward
title: VP, Gemini & Google Labs (NotebookLM, AI Studio)
bio_short: Leads Gemini, Google Labs, NotebookLM, and AI Studio at Google. Named to TIME 100 AI 2025. The most product-forward thinker at Google on shipping AI — focused on making frontier capabilities widely accessible.
